name: Basic Sourdough Starter
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve yeast in warm water using a 3-quart glass bowl.
2. Stir in milk.
3. Gradually stir in flour until smooth.
4. Cover with a towel and let stand in a warm, draft-free place until the starter begins to ferment (bubbles appear on the surface of the starter). This may take about 24 hours.
5. If the starter has not begun fermentation after 24 hours, discard it and start again.
6. If fermentation has begun, stir well, cover tightly with plastic wrap, and return to a warm, draft-free place.
7. Let stand until foamy (2-3 days).
8. When the starter is foamy, stir well and pour into a 1-quart crock or glass jar with a tight-fitting lid.
9. Store in the refrigerator.
10. The starter is ready to use when a clear liquid has risen to the top. Stir before using.
11. Use 1 cup of starter in your recipe; reserve the remaining starter.
12. To store reserved starter, add 3/4 cup milk and 3/4 cup flour. Cover and let sit at room temperature until bubbles appear (about 12 hours). Then refrigerate.
Do not use self-rising flour.
Note: Start bread at night to bake in the morning, or vice versa. Before adding milk and flour to the remaining starter, bake your bread and judge the volume.
